title: Cordemoya spinulosa
intro: Cordemoya spinulosa is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Sapindaceae and commonly known as "palo amarillo". This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cordemoya spinulosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cordemoya spinulosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make a medicinal tea, as well as a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions.
